title: hoplophytum bracteatum
intro: Hoplophytum bracteatum, also known as the bushman's poison or poison ivy of the Kalahari, is a plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's leaves and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may provide relief from pain and reduce the risk of infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or taken internally to alleviate fever and other symptoms.
